text.title: Testing (Multi-Language) — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every testing application must obey.

rule.statement: Every test has explicit Arrange, Act, Assert sections (visually or via comments).
rule.rationale: AAA structure makes the intent and failure point obvious; deviations are usually bugs.

rule.statement: Tests do not depend on order. Running any subset in any order produces the same result.
rule.rationale: Order dependency makes -k filtering and parallel execution unreliable; CI flakes.

rule.statement: Tests do not leak state. Each test starts and ends with the same observable state.
rule.rationale: Idempotence is the precondition for isolation; without it, parallelism and rerun-after-fix break.

rule.statement: Mock only at process boundaries: network, disk, clock. Do not mock internal modules.
rule.rationale: Internal mocks couple the test to the implementation; mocking the boundary tests the unit's actual contract.

rule.statement: Coverage threshold ≥80% branches on new code. Set --cov-fail-under or equivalent.
rule.rationale: A threshold turns coverage from vanity into a gate; without it, coverage drifts down.

rule.statement: Unit lane runs in <30s; integration and E2E in separate slower lanes triggered by tag or path.
rule.rationale: Fast feedback on unit work; integration/E2E only when their changes are in scope.
